runs.interpretation: Primary-earner substitution-response elasticity for earnings decile 7 in a U.S. microsimulation model
runs.reasoningSummary: Labor supply elasticities for primary earners (often sole or main) in middle-high deciles like 7 are moderate; meta-analyses suggest intensive margin around 0.2-0.5, higher than low earners due to less constraints, but bounded by empirical microsimulation calibrations.

runs.interpretation: Primary-earner substitution-response elasticity for earnings decile 7 in a U.S. microsimulation model
runs.reasoningSummary: Labor supply elasticities for primary (typically male) earners in middle-high deciles are positive but modest. Frisch elasticity around 0.2-0.5 from empirical meta-analyses; higher deciles slightly larger. Decile 7 likely ~0.3 median, wider uncertainty due to model/specification variation."

runs.interpretation: Primary-earner substitution-response elasticity for earnings decile 7 in a U.S. microsimulation model
runs.reasoningSummary: Labor supply elasticities for prime-age primary earners (decile 7, ~median to upper-middle) are positive but modest; uncompensated substitution effects around 0.1-0.5 from micro studies, higher in simulations accounting for full model responses. Uncertainty from heterogeneity and behavioral modeling assumptions.
